commands.why: Set archived_at on artifact metadata (orthogonal to trash lifecycle).
Store content-addressed artifact metadata and payload (bytes, text, or structured packet JSON).
Resolve immutable artifact metadata referenced from timelines and packets.
Search and filter immutable artifacts across the workspace.
Permanently delete a trashed artifact (human-gated).
Clear trash lifecycle fields on an artifact after an explicit restore action.
Move artifact metadata to trash with an explicit operator reason.
Clear archived_at on artifact metadata.
Soft-archive a board (orthogonal to business status; clears default list visibility).
Create a first-class card and attach it to a board.
Resolve a card through its board membership context.
List cards on one board in canonical order.
Create a durable board over topics and cards.
Resolve canonical board state and summary.
Scan durable coordination boards and lightweight summaries.
Update board metadata with optimistic concurrency.
Permanently delete a trashed board (human-gated).
Clear trash lifecycle fields on a board after an explicit restore action.
Move board to trash with an explicit operator reason.
Clear archived_at on a board (restore default list visibility).
Load the operator-facing board workspace with cards, docs, and inbox sections.
commands.output_envelope: Returns `{ board_id, board, primary_topic, cards, documents, inbox, board_summary, projection_freshness, board_summary_freshness, warnings, section_kinds, generated_at }`.

commands.why: Soft-delete a first-class card by setting archived_at (board concurrency via if_board_updated_at).
Create a card with the same body as POST /boards/{board_id}/cards, but supply board_id or board_ref here instead of a path segment. Interoperable with board-scoped create.
commands.output_envelope: Returns `{ board, card }` (same as board-scoped create).

commands.why: Resolve one first-class card by id.
Scan first-class card resources across boards.
Reposition a card within a board column using the card's first-class identity.
Update card fields, including resolution and resolution refs.

commands.summary: Permanently delete archived or trashed card
commands.why: Permanently delete an archived or trashed card (human-gated).
Clear archive or trash lifecycle fields on a card so it reappears on boards.
Load chronological evidence and related resources for one card.
commands.output_envelope: Returns `{ card, events, artifacts, cards, documents, threads }`.

commands.why: Move a card to trash with an explicit operator reason while keeping archive lifecycle distinct.
Soft-archive a document lineage (orthogonal to head revision content).
Create a canonical document lineage anchored to a typed subject ref.
Resolve a document lineage and its current head revision.
Scan canonical document lineages.
Permanently delete a trashed document (human-gated).
Clear trash state on a document after an explicit restore action.
Append a new immutable revision and advance the document head.
Resolve one immutable document revision.
Enumerate immutable revisions for one document lineage.
Move a document lineage to trash with an explicit operator reason.
Clear archived_at on a document so it returns to default visibility.
Set archived_at on an append-only event record for filtered views.
Append an event that links first-class resources and evidence through typed refs.
Inspect append-only event history across the workspace.
Clear trash state on an event after an explicit restore action.
Move event to trash with an explicit operator reason.
Clear archived_at on an event.
Suppress or clear a derived inbox item via a durable acknowledgment event.
Load the derived operator inbox generated from refs and canonical events.
Probe whether the core process is alive.
Verify storage and projection subsystems are ready for traffic.
commands.output_envelope: Returns `{ ok: true }` when the workspace is ready.

commands.why: Check compatibility between clients and core before writes.
Record structured delivery evidence anchored by `subject_ref`.
Record a structured review over a receipt anchored to the same card as subject_ref.

commands.summary: List ref edges (forward or reverse indexed lookup)
commands.why: Query the write-through ref index by source or target (mutually exclusive); reverse lookup uses target_type+target_id.
Load a compact coordination bundle (thread, recent events, key artifacts, cards, documents) for inspection and triage.
commands.output_envelope: Returns `{ thread, recent_events, key_artifacts, open_cards, documents }` plus forward-compatible fields.

commands.why: Resolve one backing thread for low-level inspection and diagnostics.
Inspect backing infrastructure threads without making them the primary planning noun.
Retrieve event history plus typed-ref expansions for one backing thread.
commands.output_envelope: Returns `{ thread, events, artifacts, topics, cards, documents }`.

commands.why: Read-only diagnostic projection that bundles context, inbox, and related-thread signals for one backing thread. Prefer topics.workspace for normal operator coordination when a topic exists.
commands.output_envelope: Returns `{ thread, related_topics, cards, documents, board_memberships, inbox, projection_freshness }`.

commands.why: Soft-archive a topic (orthogonal to business status; clears default list visibility).
Create a first-class durable topic before attaching cards, docs, or packets.
commands.agent_notes: Replay-safe when the same request key and body are reused.

commands.why: Resolve one topic and its canonical durable fields.
Scan the durable topic inventory.
Update topic state with provenance and optimistic concurrency.
Clear trash lifecycle fields on a topic after an explicit restore action.
Load chronological evidence and related resources for one topic.
commands.output_envelope: Returns `{ topic, events, artifacts, cards, documents, threads }`.

commands.why: Move topic to trash with an explicit operator reason.
Clear archived_at on a topic (restore default list visibility).

commands.summary: Get topic workspace (primary operator coordination read)
commands.why: Primary operator coordination read — load the topic workspace composed from linked cards, docs, backing threads, and inbox items. Prefer this over thread workspace for triage and planning.
commands.output_envelope: Returns `{ topic, cards, boards, documents, threads, inbox, projection_freshness, generated_at }`.
